---
title: "Conferences"
page-layout: full
description: "Conference presentations and published abstracts by Mesfin Genie."
---

::: {.page-header}
::: {.page-shell}
# Conferences

Presentations and published conference abstracts.
:::
:::

::: {.prose}
## Upcoming presentations

::: {.timeline}
::: {.timeline-item}
### International Conference on Choice Modelling

**Gold Coast, Australia · 20–22 July 2026**  
Attribute ordering, visual attention and choice behaviour in discrete choice experiments.
:::

::: {.timeline-item}
### International Academy of Health Preference Research

**Singapore · 5–7 October 2026**  
Preferences and trade-offs for dementia support programme features: a discrete choice experiment in Australia.
:::
:::

## Recent presentations

::: {.timeline}
::: {.timeline-item}
### Choice Modelling Conference / CDIC 2026

**Melbourne, Australia · 15–17 June 2026**  
*Community First or My Body First? Moral Values and Vaccine Mandate Preferences*; and *No Jab, No Access? Preferences for Vaccine Mandates in Europe and Australia*.
:::

::: {.timeline-item}
### ISPOR Annual Meeting

**Philadelphia, United States · 17–20 May 2026**  
*Choosing with Concern for the Worse-Off: Equity and Efficiency in Health Value Assessment*.
:::
:::

## Published conference abstracts

::: {.conference-list}
::: {.publication-record}
### [PCR18 Are Discrete Choice Experiment Results Robust to Omitted Attributes? Evidence from a Vaccine Study](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jval.2025.09.2491){target="_blank"}

**Mesfin Genie**; Verity Watson; Stéphane Luchini; Michaël Schwarzinger

*Value in Health*, 28, S549–S550 · [Published abstract]{.status}
:::

::: {.publication-record}
### [MSR145 Do Framing and Attribute Number Influence Choice Consistency in a Preference-Based Value Assessment of Equity and Efficiency?](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jval.2025.04.1296){target="_blank"}

**Mesfin Genie**; Surachat Ngorsuraches

*Value in Health*, 28, S302–S303 · [Published abstract]{.status}
:::

::: {.publication-record}
### [MSR75 Guidance or Misdirection? Unpacking the Role of Feedback in Health Preference Assessments](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jval.2024.03.1508){target="_blank"}

**Mesfin Genie**; Shelby Reed; Juan Gonzalez; Semra Ozdemir

*Value in Health*, 27, S274 · [Published abstract]{.status}
:::

::: {.publication-record}
### [EPH155 Relative Effectiveness of Vaccination and Non-pharmaceutical Interventions on COVID-19 Infection and Deaths in the US](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jval.2023.03.1037){target="_blank"}

Asmita P. Khatiwada; Aregawi G. Gebremariam; **Mesfin Genie**; Tim C. Lai; Nabin Poudel; Surachat Ngorsuraches

*Value in Health*, 26, S192–S193 · [Published abstract]{.status}
:::

::: {.publication-record}
### [PCR95 Decision Consistency in a Discrete Choice Experiment: Does Numeracy Skill Matter?](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jval.2023.03.1873){target="_blank"}

**Mesfin Genie**; Surachat Ngorsuraches; Nabin Poudel

*Value in Health*, 26, S329 · [Published abstract]{.status}
:::

::: {.publication-record}
### Metastatic Prostate Cancer Patients’ Attitudes towards Treatment of the Local Tumour and Metastasis Evaluative Research (IP5-MATTER)

Martin J. Connor; **Mesfin G. Genie**; Michael Gonzalez; and colleagues

*European Urology*, 79, S1217–S1218 · [Published abstract]{.status}
:::

::: {.publication-record}
### A Discrete Choice Experiment on Patients’ Time and Risk Preferences in Kidney Transplantation

C. Silvestre; **Mesfin Genie**; Antonio Nicolò; Giacomo Pasini; and colleagues

*Transplant International*, 30, 308–309 · [Published abstract]{.status}
:::
:::
:::
